title: Margin Note Shift
prompt:
Create a production-ready Margin Note Shift transition using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original transition swaps a concise Draft panel and a Reviewed panel while a small magenta margin note shifts from left to right. The incoming panel should state its phase plainly so the animation is supplemental rather than the only signal. Use a light Bento stage with white and #F4F5F6 surfaces, #ECECEE borders, 22px rounded corners, soft neutral shadow, #111111 type, and #4A4A4F supporting copy. Magenta is the only bright accent and no card gradient is permitted.

Use a native button with aria-pressed, a polite live state message, 44px minimum target, visible 3px focus ring, and normal click, Enter, and Space behavior. On activation, crossfade and translate panels by 12px over 180ms ease-out while the margin note moves with a spring at stiffness 360 and damping 28. Keep state local and deterministic, avoiding timers, random values, storage, remote assets, and network requests. Use transform and opacity rather than layout animation.

At 320px, stack the panel copy and keep the control easy to target. At larger sizes, retain a compact, centered card that grows naturally. Preserve tab order, AA contrast, and explicit phase text. With prefers-reduced-motion, swap the phase immediately and allow only a 120ms opacity fade. Return complete paste-ready client code, runnable CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML: <section class="margin-note"><button aria-pressed="false">Mark reviewed</button></section> */
.margin-note{max-width:340px;padding:20px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.margin-note::before{display:block;width:4px;height:32px;border-radius:4px;background:#d946a5;content:""}.margin-note button{min-height:44px;border:0;border-radius:12px;background:#111;color:#fff;font-weight:800}.margin-note button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:4px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.margin-note *{transition:none}}

demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [r,setR]=React.useState(false);const phase=r?'Reviewed':'Draft';return
{phase.toUpperCase()}
